name: Alocasia baginda 'Dragon Scale' Albo Variegated
origin: Species native to Borneo (Kalimantan); 'Dragon Scale' collector selection with variegation in cultivation
aboutText: Alocasia baginda 'Dragon Scale' Albo Variegated is a jaw-dropping, compact forest-floor aroid native to the tropical island of Borneo (Kalimantan). It is highly celebrated for its thick, heavily bullate, shield-like leaves that resemble reptilian scales. The foliage is a pale, silvery-green with deep, dark green contrasting venation, accented by magnificent white-to-cream albo variegation scattered over the textured surfaces. Arising from a compact underground corm, it grows on upright pinkish-to-greenish petioles and thrives in warmth and humidity with a chunky, free-draining substrate and bright indirect light.

morphology.venation: Deeply recessed, dark green primary veins
morphology.variegation: White to cream albo variegation over the lamina

fieldNotes.title: Reptilian Textures in White Marbling
fieldNotes.author: Ariod Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Alocasia baginda 'Dragon Scale' is celebrated for its thick, heavily bullate leaves that feel like leather and resemble scales. The albo variegated form introduces cream-to-white patterns across this texture. In taxonomic reviews of Borneo's endemic flora, the bullate texture is categorized as an adaptation to maximize the surface area for photosynthesis in deep limestone caves. Somatic albo variegation creates a high-contrast pattern that highlights the deep green recessed veins, making it one of the most visually striking jewel Alocasias.
